Provenance.drafts: round 1: 4 candidates (seed+0..+3), all rejected. round 2 escalation: 4 more candidates (seed+4..+7) with hardened negative (concept sheet, character sheet, turnaround, multiple views, grid layout, item icons); curated per art/pipeline/README.md
Provenance.paintoverNote: round 1 all rejected -- 2 multi-panel concept sheets, 1 two-figure comparison sheet, 1 single figure with an extra companion lamb plus an off-model bird/skull face. round 2 c2 (seed+6, offset+2 within the round) -- a single hooded lantern-bearing wood-walker holding its own staff+lantern, full body, clean silhouette; accepted despite the face being obscured by a wrapped scarf rather than showing the literal 'burlap head with button eyes' (SDXL's strong pull toward person-in-robe over wicker-scarecrow material, even after hardening).
Provenance.aiDisclosure: AI-generated (SDXL base 1.0) diffuse, background-removed via BiRefNet (cutout.py --trim); normal map derived via normalmap.py (Sobel height-from-luminance, strength 2.5) -- lit Monster world sprite per AssetSpec.NormalMap=true. Normal-map QA: normalmap.py (Sobel height-from-luminance, strength 2.5) and the Flip-Y-OFF import convention are unchanged from the prior passing waves (P006 U1/U4-U6); this wave's windowed pilot-scene sweep directly verified 6 representative samples (gloomwood-old-mossjaw, gloomwood-toll-booth, sunkencrypt-undertow, sunkencrypt-crypt-crab, props-noticeboard, props-tavern-cat) with no inversion/caving on any -- no per-asset regression risk since the script and import setting are both untouched.
